Leadership In Action — Panelist Brief

Confidential
Welcome

Welcome & Purpose

Dear Cheslyn, Mpho and Bongi,

Thank you for accepting the invitation to share your leadership insights with our senior management audience at the Bank. This brief gives you a comprehensive overview of the session – its purpose, structure, the questions you can expect, and the research that underpins them.

The aim is simple but powerful: to collect and learn from the leadership lessons you have gathered through your remarkable journeys.

Actionable, not just inspirational.

We want every leader in the room to walk out with practical takeaways they can implement immediately – on Monday morning.

Please treat this brief as a preparation guide. There are no “right answers” – we want your genuine perspectives.

Prepared by: Zoë Egling, Leadership Development Specialist

Session Overview

Session Overview

Format
40-Minute Panel
Moderator
Bongi Dlamini
Audience
Senior Management
Venue
Merchant Place, Sandton
Date
24 July 2026
Time
10h00 – 11h00
5 min
Introduction
Welcome, panelist intros, research context
18 min
Panel Discussion
17 questions to guide the moderator — not all need to be asked
11 min
Q&A Session
Audience questions
4 min
Q&Do Reflection Session
Reflective closing prompt — audience engages via Q&Do game
2 min
Closing Remarks
Synthesis, QR feedback, call to action
Panelist Profiles

Panelist Profiles

Cheslyn Jacobs

CEO • GoTyme Bank

Education: BCom Industrial Psychology (UWC), PGDip Business Management (GIBS)

Credentials: Founding team member; retail-kiosk model; 11M+ customers; profitability

Highlight: Drove TymeBank to unicorn status ($1.5B valuation) – first digital bank profitability in SA

Success comes from showing up and giving your best, no matter what. Effort and persistence matter more than results.
13+ Years

Mpho Makwana

Lead Independent Director • Invicta Holdings (JSE)

Education: B.Admin (University of Zululand), MBA (University of Stirling)

Credentials: Former Chairman Nedbank Group (2021–2023) & Eskom Holdings (2022–2023, 2009–2011)

Highlight: 33+ years; 26–28 years in executive/C-suite roles across banking, energy & industrial sectors

Motho Ke Motho ka batho (I am because you are) — being true to your roots can take you into your future, authentically.
34+ Years

Internal Moderator

Bongi Dlamini

Head: Organisational Development, Design & Business Transformation • FNB South Africa

Education: BCom (UKZN), BCom Honours Industrial Psychology (UNISA), Professional Business Coaching (GIBS)

Credentials: 13+ years at FNB South Africa; previously Absa, Parliament of RSA, Accenture

Highlight: Organisational Effectiveness Executive with expertise in OD, enterprise-wide change, leadership development, coaching & culture transformation

I am passionate about building high-performance cultures where teams and individuals are engaged, accountable and empowered to deliver results.
20+ Years
Discussion Themes

Discussion Themes & Questions

19 questions across 8 leadership themes. Select a theme below to view its questions.

Setting the tone – what leadership means and why finance leaders are uniquely positioned.
Q1Both3 min
If a five-year old asked you what leadership is, what would your response be?
Lesson: Leadership is about showing others the way, caring for people, and helping them become their best selves.
Q2Both3 min
What makes a financial mind uniquely positioned to share lessons on leadership?
Lesson: Finance leaders see the entire business landscape – strategy, risk, operations, and people.
Why AI makes human leadership more critical, not less.
Q3Cheslyn3 min
AI is making human leadership MORE critical. How do you see the relationship between technology and human leadership evolving?
Lesson: Technology amplifies human potential, but only human leaders can inspire, build trust, and navigate ambiguity with empathy.
Q4Mpho3 min
McKinsey shows AI cannot “read the room.” What human qualities become MORE valuable as organizations adopt AI?
Lesson: Empathy, emotional intelligence, and reading the room become MORE valuable as AI handles routine tasks.
Moving from control to guardrails – empowering teams.
Q5Cheslyn3 min
Command-and-control is falling flat. Leaders need “guardrails.” How did you shift your approach moving from CCO to CEO?
Lesson: Great leaders set guardrails and values, then empower teams to decide within that framework.
Q6Mpho4 min
You chaired Eskom during load shedding, Nedbank during uncertainty. How do you create guardrails for teams navigating volatility?
Lesson: In crisis, leaders provide the balcony view – frameworks and perspective amid chaos.
The shift from back-office to strategic architect.
Q7Both4 min
Deloitte: 57% of finance leaders now lead enterprise strategy. How have you seen this play out?
Lesson: Finance evolved from scorekeeper to strategic architect.
Q8Cheslyn3 min
63% of finance teams use AI, but only 21% see value. What’s the gap?
Lesson: Adoption without strategy creates waste – value requires disciplined execution.
Trust, self-awareness, and organisational alignment.
Q9Both3 min
How have you built trust as a leader? What specific things do you do or say?
Lesson: Trust is built through consistency, vulnerability, and follow-through.
Q10Mpho3 min
McKinsey identifies six mindsets. Which has been hardest to develop, and why?
Lesson: Self-awareness about gaps is a strength – the best leaders work on blind spots.
Q11Cheslyn4 min
“Organisational alignment” — scaling TymeBank to 11M+ customers. How did you keep alignment?
Lesson: Alignment through clarity of purpose, repeated communication, and empowering leaders.
Where financial services leaders excel and struggle.
Q12Both4 min
HBR: 10,000+ executives – strong on strategy, weak on mobilising and agility. Does that resonate?
Lesson: Technical brilliance without people leadership creates a ceiling.
Q13Mpho3 min
Purpose-driven orgs outperform by 42%. How do you embed purpose as lived behaviour?
Lesson: Purpose must be lived, not laminated.
Talent, rituals, resilience, and digital transformation in SA.
Q14Cheslyn3 min
Talent retention – you scaled a digital bank from zero. Secret to keeping great people?
Lesson: People don’t leave companies – they leave leaders.
Q15Both3 min
One ritual or practice your team would say makes a real difference?
Lesson: Leadership is built in daily habits, not annual strategy sessions.
Q16Mpho3 min
82% of African CEOs confident in growth. What gives African leaders this resilience?
Lesson: Resilience forged through adversity is a competitive advantage.
Q17Both4 min
Digital transformation is “non-negotiable.” Biggest mistake leaders make?
Lesson: DT fails when treated as tech instead of a people project.
Monday Morning advice and the enduring relevance of Ubuntu.
Q18Cheslyn2 min
One piece of advice — one thing to do differently starting Monday?
Lesson: The best advice is specific, actionable, and personal.
Q19Mpho2 min
Ubuntu — “Motho ke motho ka batho.” How does Ubuntu remain relevant in 2026?
Lesson: Ubuntu is timeless – the most human leaders will be the most impactful.
Monday Morning

The Monday Morning Challenge

The golden thread. Our audience needs practical actions. For every insight, we want a leader to say: “I can do that on Monday.”
“What is ONE thing a leader could do differently starting Monday as a result of hearing my answer?”
Human Leadership
Start one conversation about how AI could free up time – ask your team what they’d do with it.
Command to Context
Identify one decision to delegate with clear guardrails. Hand it over Monday.
Finance as Strategy
Invite your finance partner to challenge assumptions, not report numbers.
Six Mindsets
Choose your weakest CEO mindset. One action this week to strengthen it.
Critical Gaps
“What could I do to make transformation feel less like a crisis?” – and listen.
SA Context
15-minute conversation with your highest-flight-risk person about aspirations, not KPIs.
Research

Research Foundation

McKinsey
Human Leadership in the AI Age (Jan 2026)
AI makes human leadership more critical. Only humans set aspirations, build trust, and “read the room.”
25% higher engagement | 30% more innovation
McKinsey
CEO Excellence Research
Six mindsets: directional thinking, organisational alignment, mobilising through leaders, board engagement, stakeholder connection, personal effectiveness.
6 Mindsets of Exceptional CEOs
Deloitte
Finance Trends 2026
57% of finance leaders lead strategy. 63% use AI, only 21% see clear value.
57% lead strategy | 21% see value
Harvard Business Review
Financial Services Leadership
10,000+ executives: strong on strategy, weak on mobilising, transformation, agility.
Purpose-driven orgs outperform by 42%
KPMG
Africa Financial Services 2026
82% of African CEOs confident in growth. Resilience built through adversity.
82% CEOs confident
FAnews
The 2026 Shift
Digital transformation non-negotiable in SA. AI offers game-changing advantage.
Non-negotiable
Activities

Audience Activities

Q&Do Game

Moderator uses the Q&Do game to pose a final reflective question. The game surfaces a closing prompt that sparks real-time audience reflection and dialogue.

QR Code Feedback

QR code links to feedback form – captures resonance and attendance.

No action required from you.

Tips & Contact

Practical Tips

1
Keep responses to 3–4 minutes. Concise answers ensure coverage.
2
Lead with stories, not theory. Anecdotes resonate more than principles.
3
Anchor in Monday Morning actions. “Here’s what that looks like in practice…”
4
Be authentic and candid. Vulnerability builds trust.
5
Expect follow-up probes. Designed to deepen the conversation.
6
Engage with your co-panelist. Best moments come from genuine dialogue.
7
Q&A is unscripted. 11 minutes of spontaneous audience questions.
8
No slides needed. Just your perspective and willingness to share.
Contact
Zoë Egling
Leadership Development Specialist
zoe.egling@fnb.co.za

We look forward to an inspiring, practical, and memorable conversation.
Thank you for sharing your leadership lessons with our community.

Q&Do Game

⚡ Q & Do — Leadership Lessons Alive

A live, interactive dice game that turns today’s leadership insights into powerful reflection questions. Click the dice to roll — land on a lesson, explore a question, challenge your thinking.

ⓘ How to Play ▼
  1. Click the dice to roll. Each face represents one of six leadership themes from today’s session.
  2. Read the question that appears below — these are drawn from research and the panelists’ real leadership experiences.
  3. Reflect & discuss: Use the follow-up probe to deepen the conversation at your table or in your own thinking.
  4. Roll again to explore a different theme. The game cycles through 40 unique prompts across all six lessons.
